queue.name: ID0399: Information Conservation and Holographic Preservation

master_row.title: Information Conservation and Holographic Preservation
master_row.canonical_statement: Information currents and entropies obey four complementary conservation principles: Noether conservation of an information current $J^{\mu}_{\mathrm{info}}$, Liouville preservation of phase-space volume, unitary conservation of total probability, and the holographic entropy bound limiting information density by boundary area. The holographic preservation theorem asserts that information remains preserved through dimensional collapse at a TZP by topological protection.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a differential equation relating U, I.
